author: @Buzzhumma
text: noise cancelling headphones would be a great new topic!HAHA Actually Rob did create some vibration energy harvesters so i see where this is going !

author: @roberthopgood1894
text:
EXACTLY how did you do the support to actually HOLD THE BIKE??????????
you REALLY need to do a step by step instruction or this is useless
You could also have used more angle pieces

author: @truthseeker3907
text: Keep Up The Good Work Luke and Thumbs up! :)

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: I hate being negative, but, Blimey, Carbon Fibre, Epoxy Resin, ( nasty, and lasts thousands of years in the environment) Expanding foam. Why not Bolt, Weld, Rivet etc the bars together! Had to give thumbs down for first time on this channel . ✊🏽🌎✊🏽🌻✌🏽

author: @BushImports
text: What's next, are you going to hook an alternator or some form of generator to it? That part looks like it turned out great.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you! Yeah pt3 is going to be cool!

author: @Nick_Tag
text: I’ve had a dream of turning a turbo trainer into an input for Apple 3d maps or Google maps Street view so I can cycle streets around the world, either in virtual reality or a nice big screen in front of me, would be super cool 😎

author: @sofa-lofa4241
text:
Carbon fibre?... That's some hi-tech bodgery going on there
Next project... Unobtainium!

It would be cool if you spoke about your experience working with C. F., the trials and tribulations... And the splinters!
It would be a very interesting video and useful too as I don't think there are many people who have tried it at home.

author: @alanclarke4646
text: There's a fair amount of lateral instability when you're pedalling.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: It felt alot more stable then it looked on video, I was thinking about reinforcing the uprights so they don't move at all in the next video though.

author: @DiyEcoProjects
text:
Awesome video Luke, nice one, well done. 
We like your cheery attitude

author: @colleenforrest7936
text:
I like the method you used to create the mount.  Love to see a more detailed video just on that later (or did you do that before? I'm coming in at the second video)

Also, what's nice is this will easily convert back to a working bike with a quick change of the rear axil.

author: @jeffreyrood8755
text: Hey Luke question, what is the scrubbing sound? Was the tire rubbing the board for tension or something like that?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hi Jeffery, I think it is the friction of the tire rubbing against the rollers? or it could be the rollers rubbing against the base, I do need to put spacers there. :)

author: @colouroboros9993
text: looks awesome just wish you'd show us the process of making them instead of just talking about it

author: @MDMAx
text:
All it needs is an energy converter of some sort and you'll have a bike trainer. 
Also, the kid is good. He tries to imitate you with flying colours and feels unlike himself yet, but it will pass. He's comprehensive and precise. The two communication qualities I have observed in many successful people in my life. 
Keep the vids coming, lads!

author: @cptrikester2671
text: Ok. Wondering if it would have been easier to use the back of a bike for the two uprights.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is a good point! I'm too far in the project now to make a change like this. But a great idea!

author: @experimentalprogramming1758
text: Very nice. Personally prefer to stand while on a bike, after reading and realizing it can be unhealthy to sit on one for very long.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
You have to take those studies with a grain of salt.
The next one will likely claim that it's unhealthy to ride a bike while standing up.

author: @chipheadnet
text: Robert,   did you find the fountain of youth?   You look sooo much younger that you did yesterday!

replies.author: @creepingjesus
replies.text: Luke here...I can become any age I want. Yours, RMS.

replies.author: @BushImports
replies.text: That's a good one, I'm sure he would not object to that. I would be thrilled to find the fountain of youth, I think most of us who are no longer young would..
